reason: Persisting unprovenanced operator-norm->pointwise step (round-2 ch-f11cea6e fix only HALF applied). The crux conversion of node 1.2's OPERATOR-NORM convergence R_N->R into POINTWISE convergence R_N z -> R z (needed at z = x, y, x+y, sx for both additivity and homogeneity) rests entirely on the bound ||R_N z - R z|| <= ||R_N - R|| ||z||. The revision NOW writes this bound and makes R_N z -> R z explicit (good), but still provenances NOTHING for the bound: the statement ends 'Uses node 1.2' only. The bound ||Tz|| <= ||T|| ||z|| is the defining consequence of the INDUCED OPERATOR-NORM sup-definition ||T||:=sup{||Ta||:||a||<=1}, which is established ONLY in node 1.1.2 (whose own body uses ||TUa||<=||T|| ||Ua||). Node 1.2 establishes operator-norm convergence and ||R-I||<=C eta; it does NOT establish the operator-action bound ||Tz||<=||T|| ||z||, so 'Uses node 1.2' cannot cover this step, and the dependency cannot be inherited transitively through 1.2's own citation of 1.1.2. Round-2 ch-f11cea6e prescribed exactly: 'cite node 1.1.2 for ||R_N(z)-R(z)|| <= ||R_N-R|| ||z||'. That citation is still absent. Per the project rule 'no standard facts', the operator-norm->pointwise bound must name node 1.1.2 (induced-operator-norm contractivity). Fix: add 'node 1.1.2' as the in-text provenance for ||R_N z - R z|| <= ||R_N - R|| ||z|| (alongside 'Uses node 1.2').
